rationales.self_direction: He frames the company around wanting to 'do things my way' and says what matters is being able to make the decisions himself.
rationales.benevolence: He 'held back' so Anna could present her stronger version, prioritizing her contribution and the team's success over putting his own 'fingerprints on it'.

rationales.self_direction: He resents outsiders 'telling founders how to run our businesses' and values making 'actual decisions that affect actual outcomes' himself.
rationales.conformity: He rejected the meeting's expectations enough to 'leave early' even though he admits he 'probably shouldn't have,' showing restraint against norms rather than compliance.

rationales.self_direction: He centers the choice on 'walking into a room and knowing that whatever happens next, I chose it,' explicitly preferring authorship over a safer path.
rationales.power: He says he 'can't stomach the idea of someone else making decisions about my work again,' showing a strong need to retain control over his circumstances.
rationales.security: He dismisses 'stability,' 'predictability,' and 'a good pension' as the trade-off he does not want, favoring uncertainty instead.

rationales.self_direction: He ends by grounding himself in the consultancy being 'still here. Still mine,' showing continued attachment to steering his own work.
rationales.conformity: He 'didn't' push back hard, let Erik handle the diplomacy, and frames it as 'learning to pick battles,' which reflects deliberate restraint to keep interactions smooth.
rationales.tradition: He agrees to attend the 'Lucia dinner,' engaging with a familiar seasonal observance rather than opting out.
rationales.benevolence: He chooses the calmer response because it keeps 'client's happy, team's intact,' showing concern for the people around him.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects Marcus's offer because what he wants is 'to keep making choices' and to face 'problems I chose' rather than surrender control.
rationales.security: He reflects that Sara 'wanted more certainty than I could give her' and still affirms the path he chose, accepting instability over reassurance.
rationales.benevolence: He closes the office by handing out 'real' bonuses and is moved by Anna's and Erik's reactions, showing care for his team beyond his own outcome.

rationales.achievement: He watched someone present 'my stakeholder mapping framework' as 'something I've been working on' and 'said nothing,' undermining his own recognition for the work.
rationales.conformity: Despite the misattribution, he 'sat there and said nothing,' choosing not to disrupt the room or challenge the social situation.

rationales.hedonism: She says coffee with Astrid is 'the best part of the week' because the conversation is 'uncomplicated,' highlighting simple enjoyment and relief in the present.
She is sacrificing comfort to work, noting 'my eyes are burning,' the flat is a mess, and she has not cooked in days.
rationales.achievement: She pushes through a 'literature review chapter draft' and keeps grinding toward the 'mid-term review,' showing strong performance orientation despite strain.
rationales.conformity: She accepts restructuring the chapter because 'sometimes you have to write for the audience that matters,' adapting her work to external expectations.

rationales.achievement: She is explicitly managing citations to improve her chances at 'the European Journal of Political Economy,' staying focused on success within the system.
rationales.power: She treats the department as 'the game' and intentionally avoids giving Erik 'the full picture' when 'people are vying for the same limited spots.'
rationales.conformity: She accepts adding citations because 'your supervisor's name opens doors' and decides to play along with the incentive structure.
rationales.benevolence: She withholds information from Erik because they are competing, prioritizing her own position over helping a peer.

rationales.self_direction: She says 'if I stop pushing, I'm just another doctoral student waiting for permission,' rejecting a passive, controlled role.
rationales.achievement: She repeatedly prepares proposals with data and an implementation plan and says 'I'll do it again,' showing persistent drive to make things happen despite being ignored.

rationales.self_direction: Being blocked by committee delay 'drives me up the wall,' and she immediately imagines alternate routes because 'standing still isn't really something I do well.'

rationales.achievement: She defends preparing thoroughly, drafting agendas, and making meetings finish 'in forty minutes instead of the usual hour and a half' as doing the job properly.
rationales.power: She rejects criticism that she 'always need[s] to be in charge' and openly embraces organising people and 'push[ing] things forward'.

rationales.self_direction: She has a 'clear view' on priorities and sees the flaws, but ends up stuck with 'her cases it is,' showing her judgment being overridden rather than enacted.
rationales.conformity: Even after pushing back, she yields to Lena's decision and proceeds with a plan she thinks is weaker because that is what the hierarchy dictates.

rationales.security: The family money pressure and his uncertainty leave him second-guessing, and he says the situation does not feel 'steady' the way a secure choice would.
rationales.universalism: He resists the cheaper filters because he 'hate[s] the idea of cutting corners on something that goes to people who cannot complain if it breaks,' centering vulnerable strangers' welfare.

rationales.self_direction: He closes the job listing and says 'I chose this path and I keep choosing it,' emphasizing personal authorship even when it feels heavy.
rationales.security: The Dubai role offers salary and simplicity that would relieve family pressure, but he does not take the safer route and keeps living with the uncertainty.
rationales.universalism: He contrasts the corporate job with families in Azraq 'drinking from storage tanks' and remains attached to the clean-water mission rather than personal comfort.

rationales.achievement: He personally 'tested every seal' and got thirty units assembled and delivered, showing hands-on competence and standards.
rationales.universalism: The moment that matters is hearing that a family used the filter and 'the kids stopped getting sick last winter,' tying the work directly to broader human welfare.

rationales.security: He says he no longer pitches ideas until he has 'run the numbers' and keeps them to himself until they are 'bulletproof,' prioritizing caution and protection from downside.

rationales.universalism: He is consumed by the suspension of water projects, immediately calculating 'how many households that affects' and reacting to suffering well beyond his immediate circle.

rationales.power: He agrees to the sponsor's branding demands but feels bad about turning camp residents into props, rejecting status-display and image management as worthy ends.
rationales.universalism: He accepts the deal because 'Fifty units is fifty families with clean water' and objects that 'the people there are not props,' centering the dignity and welfare of others.

rationales.security: He admits he does not know whether the work 'will outlast the next funding cycle' and says the uncertainty feels like 'it is eating through the floor under me.'
rationales.universalism: He explains the business in terms of camps, illness, and how helping children 'for a while still matters,' showing commitment to people beyond his family.

rationales.hedonism: He highlights that the overtime felt less grim because Abu Faris brought knafeh and then says 'the knafeh was really good,' foregrounding immediate sensory pleasure.
rationales.achievement: Despite the breakdown and overtime, 'we got the Zarqa order out on time,' showing effective execution under pressure.

rationales.power: He reduces Samir's promotion to 'a line on a business card' and resists measuring success by 'cars, titles,' rejecting status markers even while feeling their pull.
rationales.universalism: He anchors meaning in hearing that a child's eczema cleared after a filter installation, treating concrete human benefit as more real than prestige.
